A STRIP MALL WAS DAMAGED BY A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM. IN ONE STORE...A FENCE WAS BLOWN THROUGH A ROLL-UP DOOR AT THE BACK OF THE STORE. THE WIND THEN TRAVELED FROM BACK TO FRONT INSIDE THE STORE...REARRANGING THE MERCHANDISE...AND FINALLY BLOWING OUT THE FRONT DOOR. ACROSS THE STREET...PART OF THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF A DISCOUNT STORE. TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN...AS WAS PART OF A BILLBOARD. STORAGE BUILDINGS WERE OVERTURNED. MUCH OF THE DAMAGE WAS NEAR AIRPORT ROAD AND SOUTH MOORE ROAD.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY JUST SOUTH OF REDFIELD. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT A MILE AND A HALF. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE...WITH SOME STORAGE SHEDS DESTROYED. A NUMBER OF TREES WERE ALSO DOWNED IN THE AREA. MORE DETAILS CONCERNING THIS STORM WILL BE SENT ON SUNDAY.

THE WESTBOUND LANE OF HIGHWAY 36 EAST HAS CAVED IN DUE TO A WASHOUT...AND HAS BEEN BARRICADED.
BISHOP PARK FLOODED IN BRYANT.
FOUR HOMES WERE DAMAGED NEAR HALLEY. THE DAMAGE WAS GENERALLY MINOR ACCORDING TO THE REPORT.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS CONFIRMED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN SATURDAY NIGHT. THE STORM CROSSED THE CHICOT AND DESHA COUNTY LINE TWICE DAMAGING FOUR HOMES IN HALLEY. MORE DETAILS CONCERNING THIS TORNADO WILL BE SENT ON MONDAY.
